description: Cross-repo data contracts (F-CONTRACT-FIXTURE-SAMPLING). The PRODUCER is the canonical source of truth for each record's shape. Consumer tests MUST run against fixtures sampled from the producer's real output — never against a fixture hand-authored to the consumer's expected shape. That anti-pattern caused the 2026-05-24 /control-room/framework 13-day silent regression (producer emitted `timestamp`, consumer expected `event.ts`, both test suites used the consumer's wrong field).

contracts.name: gate-coverage
contracts.notes: The 2026-05-24 W16 incident contract. Canonical key is `timestamp` (NOT `event.ts`).

contracts.name: state-json-schema
contracts.notes: fitme-story's pre-build sync mirrors a subset of state.json into src/data/features. NOTE: the identity field is NOT invariant — older features use `feature`, newer use `feature_name`, fitme-story-native reverse-mirrors use `name`. The aggregator surfaced this on first run; required_keys is therefore the truly-universal subset {current_phase, framework_version}. Identity-key normalization is tracked separately.

contracts.name: audit-log
contracts.notes: Canonical producer is fitme-story. FT2 holds a daily mirror; the FT2 substrate samples that mirror with provenance=mirror. The canonical fitme-story-side sample is produced by the fitme-story follow-up PR.
